acceptance_targets: [chapter_04_craft_001] Pass if a cold reader of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:324-335 can tell Jet has his own urgent reason to chase the ship, but cannot yet infer that a dead informant's credentials were used on his file; fail if the revised passage still names an informant, dead access, or a synonymous reveal.

[chapter_04_prose_001] Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:294-300 reads either as clean Spike-observed inference or as an unmistakable new focalizer for at least a full paragraph before interior access; fail if the revised text still flatly tells the reader what Faye hates or what Jet knows without a clear POV shift.

[chapter_04_prose_002] Pass if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:348-390 contains at least three separate interior beats from Spike, including one after engine preheat starts and one before Jet's request at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:390; fail if the scene can still be read as a long surface-only exchange with Spike's next real interiority deferred until after the promise.

[chapter_04_dialogue_001] Pass if, in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:290-342, at least two characters' motives must be inferred from messy pressured speech rather than a complete self-summary, and no four-speaker run reads as four consecutive motive speeches; fail if the revised scene still presents the crew's reasons as neat one-by-one declarations.

[chapter_04_prose_003] Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:51, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:332, the negation-correction template appears no more than twice and the revised beats read with clearly different sentence movement on reread; fail if the same corrective pivot still dominates three or more of those spots.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:324-335 can tell Jet has his own urgent reason to chase the ship, but cannot yet infer that a dead informant's credentials were used on his file; fail if the revised passage still names an informant, dead access, or a synonymous reveal.
findings.evidence: Jet tells the crew, "Somebody used a dead informant's access to stick a bounty on me that shouldn't exist," at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334.
findings.problem: This prematurely exposes Jet's dead-informant clue, contradicting the continuity sheet's knowledge-state that keeps that access-path fact hidden from the crew until chapter 11. The chapter can still give Jet a private reason to pursue without cashing the later reveal early.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the bridge confession at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:324 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334 so Jet names only the actionable pressure already safe to surface now: a dormant bounty, an old file, or a private mess that has become mobile again. Do not mention a dead informant, informant access, or any equivalent code-path explanation in this chapter.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:294-300 reads either as clean Spike-observed inference or as an unmistakable new focalizer for at least a full paragraph before interior access; fail if the revised text still flatly tells the reader what Faye hates or what Jet knows without a clear POV shift.
findings.evidence: The narration states "Faye hated beats like that" at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:294 and "He absorbed that like a blow he knew he had earned" at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:300.
findings.problem: In a chapter otherwise held close to Spike, these sentences enter Faye's and Jet's inner states without a marked POV turn. That breaks the style-bible requirement for close third control and makes the scene's camera feel sloppy rather than intentionally ensemble.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:294 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:300, either rephrase through Spike's observations of face, posture, delay, and tone, or make a clearly sustained POV handoff before naming another character's private knowledge. Do not let single throwaway sentences slip inside another head.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised span at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:348-390 contains at least three separate interior beats from Spike, including one after engine preheat starts and one before Jet's request at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:390; fail if the scene can still be read as a long surface-only exchange with Spike's next real interiority deferred until after the promise.
findings.evidence: From snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:348 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:390, the bridge sequence runs for well over ten consecutive dialogue/action paragraphs before Spike's next internal beat returns at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:392.
findings.problem: That long exterior stretch drains the focalizer's interior pressure and makes the bridge section read like screenplay coverage. The cost is highest right before Spike lies to Jet: the chapter has moved his body through the scene, but not kept the reader close enough to the choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the bridge run from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:348 to snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:390 to add Spike's internal experience at pressure points: when Jet preheats, when the launch becomes a fact, and immediately before Jet asks for honesty about the band. Use thought, sensation, or self-assessment, not just extra blocking.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:290-342, at least two characters' motives must be inferred from messy pressured speech rather than a complete self-summary, and no four-speaker run reads as four consecutive motive speeches; fail if the revised scene still presents the crew's reasons as neat one-by-one declarations.
findings.evidence: The bridge argument gives Faye a clean motive statement at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:290, Spike one at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:302, Ed one at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:312, and Jet one at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334.
findings.problem: The scene turns each character's reason for pursuit into a tidy, self-explanatory speech beat. Instead of sounding overheard and pressured, the exchange starts sounding arranged so the chapter can lay out four motives in order. That is exactly the kind of balanced mini-speech polish the constitution warns against.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:290, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:302, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:312, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:334, roughen at least two motive reveals so they arrive through interruption, partial admission, deflection, or a wrong first answer before the usable reason emerges. Keep the information, but stop letting everyone summarize themselves cleanly.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:51, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:332, the negation-correction template appears no more than twice and the revised beats read with clearly different sentence movement on reread; fail if the same corrective pivot still dominates three or more of those spots.
findings.evidence: The chapter reuses the same negation-correction construction at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:19 ("It wasn't because of memory"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:51 ("It wasn't a ledger now"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71 ("It wasn't paranoia"), and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:332 ("It wasn't louder. It was tighter").
findings.problem: Once that corrective sentence shape appears this many times in one chapter, it stops reading as chosen emphasis and starts reading as autopilot. The repeated rhythm flattens otherwise strong prose by teaching the reader exactly how the sentence will pivot.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the local passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:19, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:51, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:71, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:332 so at least two of them render the idea directly instead of through an "It wasn't X / it was Y" correction. Vary syntax and rhetorical approach rather than swapping synonyms into the same frame.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel's premise demands tonal range that most novels avoid: genuine slapstick comedy in the same book as genuine grief, syndicate violence alongside domestic warmth, existential identity crisis alongside arguments about noodles. The risk is not in the dark material — it's in the tonal juxtaposition. Commit to both registers fully. A comic scene should be actually funny, not wryly amusing. A grief scene should be actually painful, not tastefully sad. The Bebop register earns its power by refusing to choose between comedy and tragedy — they coexist in the same room, the same conversation, the same character. Do not smooth the transitions between registers. Let them be jarring when the material is jarring. Let a joke land in the middle of a painful conversation if that's what the character would do. Let a painful truth arrive during a comic scene without the comedy stopping to make room for it. The creative risk is trust: trust that the reader can hold both, trust that tonal instability is a form of emotional honesty, not a failure of control.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure. When a scene arrives at a moment of physical, emotional, or moral consequence, the prose must stay present — naming, rendering, inhabiting. Summary, vagueness, and decorative circumlocution at the point of impact are disallowed. If a character is hurt, drunk, broke, grieving, or afraid, render the specific texture of that experience. 'She was devastated' is not writing. 'She found his grocery list on the fridge' is writing. The Bebop world is broke, dangerous, and populated by people who have lost things they cannot get back. The prose must honor that.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific precision. The Bebop crew lives in contractions; full forms should feel conspicuous when they appear.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Consecutive chapters must differ in at least two of: scene count, dialogue density, pacing, primary emotional register, and structural shape. A dialogue-heavy chapter should be followed by one with more interiority or action. A multi-scene chapter should be followed by one with fewer scenes or a different temporal rhythm. Vary paragraph length distribution across chapters — some chapters should run long and flowing, others should be built from shorter, harder units. The novel's rhythm at the chapter level should feel like a jazz set: different tempos, different keys, with throughlines that connect them.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, vernacular-adjacent, unsentimental. The narration should sound like an extension of the characters' world — working-class, spacefaring, slightly retro-future. Avoid elevated literary diction unless the passage specifically calls for it (rare). Avoid clinical or academic language. The narrator is not above the characters; the narrator is in the room with them. Latinate vocabulary should be rare and purposeful. Anglo-Saxon roots preferred for physical description.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating by chapter or scene as indicated in the outline. The camera sits behind the POV character's eyes — we see what they see, hear their internal register in the narration, and do not access other characters' thoughts except through observed behavior. When the chapter is ensemble, the camera moves between characters within scenes but does not overlay multiple interiorities simultaneously. Maintain enough distance that the narrator's voice is present alongside the character's — this is not stream of consciousness but inhabited third person.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Jazz-inflected variation. Sentences should vary aggressively in length and structure — short declaratives next to longer compound sentences, fragments where they create rhythmic force (but never elliptical clipping). The Bebop register favors syncopation: beat expectations set up and then broken. Avoid metronomic alternation (short-long-short-long). Let passages breathe with their own rhythms. Action sequences should accelerate sentence pace. Reflective passages should slow and lengthen. Transitions between registers should be controlled, not abrupt.
